ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for pandan bread with butter; mapped to the broader 'bread food product' class as the closest defensible ancestor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for pandan-flavoured bread with butter; A000E (Bread and similar products) is the closest defensible category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept identified for pandan bread with butter; left null to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving is one slice of pandan bread with butter, approximately 40 g, as commonly consumed at a Singaporean breakfast or tea break.

nutrient_source.title: Pandan Bread with Butter — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Foodo database entry for Pandan Bread with Butter (cr_id F05020072). Saturated fat is estimated from the butter component (~9.5 g/100 g) as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this composite dish; this estimate is consistent with USDA FoodData Central data for butter (NDB 01001, ~51% saturated fat of total fat) applied proportionally to the reported total fat of 17.57 g/100 g.

gi_source.title: White bread GI reference — University of Sydney GI Database; analogous estimate applied to pandan bread
gi_source.source_match_notes: Pandan bread is not individually listed in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 75 is derived from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for white wheat bread (GI ~75, high category), which is the closest approved analogous food given that pandan bread uses a refined wheat flour base with similar starch gelatinisation characteristics. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (75 × 37.94 × 0.40) / 100 = 11.4 for a 40 g slice.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium (266 mg/100 g) combined with high saturated fat from butter (~9.5 g/100 g) contributing to arterial stiffness
hypertension.guidance: Sodium at 266 mg/100 g is below the 400 mg/100 g high-concern threshold, but the saturated fat content from butter (~9.5 g/100 g) can contribute to arterial stiffness and elevated blood pressure over time; individuals with hypertension should limit butter quantity and avoid adding salty toppings or condiments.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High GI (75) refined wheat flour base with 37.94 g/100 g carbohydrate and saturated fat from butter worsening insulin resistance
type2_diabetes.guidance: The high GI (75) and 37.94 g available carbohydrate per 100 g produce a significant postprandial glucose spike; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit to one slice (40 g), pair with a protein source to blunt glycaemic response, and consider requesting wholemeal pandan bread to lower the GI, per ADA dietary guidelines.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (~9.5 g/100 g estimated from butter) and cholesterol (43.45 mg/100 g) elevating LDL-C risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The estimated saturated fat of ~9.5 g/100 g from butter substantially exceeds the 5 g/100 g cardiovascular concern threshold and, combined with 43.45 mg/100 g cholesterol, poses a meaningful LDL-cholesterol-raising risk; individuals with cardiovascular disease should replace butter with an unsaturated fat spread such as avocado or extra-virgin olive oil and limit portion size to one slice per occasion.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat ~9.5 g/100 g from butter exceeds the 5 g/100 g RA threshold and promotes pro-inflammatory cytokine activity
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Butter contributes an estimated 9.5 g/100 g saturated fat, which exceeds the 5 g/100 g RA threshold and promotes TNF-alpha and IL-6 inflammatory pathways; individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should replace butter with avocado spread or extra-virgin olive oil to maintain an anti-inflammatory fat profile.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pandan bread with butter is a popular Singaporean breakfast and tea-time staple consisting of sliced soft white bread flavoured with pandan (screwpine) leaf extract, spread with a generous layer of salted or unsalted butter. The bread has a characteristically light green hue, a subtly sweet and fragrant pandan aroma, and a soft, pillowy crumb texture. It is commonly sold at kopitiams (traditional coffee shops), bakeries, and hawker centres across Singapore and Malaysia. Nutritionally, per 100 g it provides 348 kcal, 10.12 g protein, 17.57 g total fat (of which approximately 9.5 g is saturated fat from butter), 37.94 g carbohydrate, 3.06 g dietary fibre, 266 mg sodium, 281 mg calcium, and 3.97 mg iron. The high GI (~75) and significant saturated fat content make it a moderate-to-high concern food for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis. The calcium content (281 mg/100 g) is notably high for a bread product, likely reflecting milk or milk powder in the formulation. A typical serving is one slice (~40 g), providing approximately 139 kcal and 11.4 glycaemic load units.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F05020072, confidence 0.95), which is the primary approved source for Singaporean dishes. Saturated fat is estimated from the butter component using USDA FoodData Central butter data as a secondary approved source. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using white bread as the closest approved analogue.
notes: Fields set to -1 and missing-data basis: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, and trans_fat_g set to -1 because the HPB Foodo database entry for F05020072 does not report a detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite pandan bread with butter product, and no sufficiently close match providing these values was found in USDA FoodData Central or FAO/INFOODS SEA as of 2026-04-16. water_g set to -1 because moisture content for this composite product is not reported in the HPB Foodo database and no approved-source equivalent was identified. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, and selenium_mcg set to -1 because the HPB Foodo database entry for F05020072 does not include a full micronutrient panel for this composite dish, and no sufficiently close match providing these values was found in USDA FoodData Central or FAO/INFOODS SEA as of 2026-04-16. Saturated fat of 9.5 g/100 g is an estimate derived by applying the USDA FoodData Central butter saturated fat proportion (~51% of total fat) to the HPB-reported total fat of 17.57 g/100 g, consistent with the butter-spread preparation described. Potassium is reported as 4.0 mg/100 g per HPB, which is unusually low and may reflect a data gap in the source; this value is preserved as reported. GI of 75 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database white bread reference, as pandan bread is not individually listed. All core macros (protein_g 10.12, fat_g 17.57, carbohydrate_g 37.94) are non-zero and sourced from HPB; no zero-macro justification is required. Added 'Ban lan niu you mian bao' to flat aliases array to ensure romanized entry from aliases_by_language.romanized is present verbatim.
